name: Auto-sampler
description:
This algorithm allows you to automatically create multi-sampled instruments in a format that can be used by the Poly Multisample algorithm, by triggering an external synth (by MIDI or CV/gate) and recording the resulting audio.

You can set the range of notes to be sampled, and the step size (e.g. every note, every fourth note etc.). You can also choose to sample multiple velocity levels per note, and multiple round-robins of each note.

The samples are stored on the MicroSD card, in a folder within the root ‘samples’ folder.
gui_description: While sampling, the display shows a level meter, the filename of the sample currently being recorded, and the elapsed time. It also shows a gauge of the pressure on the MicroSD card.

parameters.name: Folder name
parameters.description: The name of the folder to create when starting a recording. If the folder already exists, a number will be appended to make the new name unique.

parameters.name: Bit depth
parameters.description: Chooses the bit depth for recording. The options are 16, 24, or 32 bit.

parameters.name: Normalisation
parameters.description: Sets the input voltage that corresponds to full-scale in the recorded files. The options are 10V or 12V.

parameters.name: Note Step
parameters.description: The number of notes to increment by after each sample (e.g. ‘1’ samples every note, ‘12’ samples every octave etc.).

parameters.name: Vel switches
parameters.description: The number of velocity switch layers to sample.

parameters.name: Min velocity
parameters.description: The minimum velocity value to use when sampling with velocity switch layers.

parameters.name: Max velocity
parameters.description: The maximum velocity value to use when sampling with velocity switch layers, or the fixed value to use if not using velocity switches.

parameters.name: Round robins
parameters.description: The number of round-robins to sample.

parameters.name: Length
parameters.description: The note length (i.e. how long the gate is held high, or between MIDI note on and note off).

parameters.name: Gap
parameters.description: The time to keep recording after the note is released.

parameters.name: Latency
parameters.description: Adjusts for latency. Set the Latency parameter to something close to the value shown in the display during a Test note to avoid silence at the start of recorded samples.

parameters.name: Preview note
parameters.description: Sets the MIDI note number to use when previewing.

parameters.name: Test
parameters.description: When on, triggers a note so you can test the timing parameters (Length and Gap) and the latency.

input_ports.name: Audio Input
input_ports.description: Audio signals from an external synth to be recorded.

input_ports.name: Left/mono input
input_ports.description: The left channel input to record.

input_ports.name: Right input
input_ports.description: The right channel input, if recording in stereo.

output_ports.name: CV Output
output_ports.description: Pitch CV to trigger an external synth.

output_ports.name: Gate Output
output_ports.description: Gate signal to trigger an external synth.

output_ports.name: MIDI Output
output_ports.description: MIDI data to trigger an external synth.

output_ports.name: MIDI output
output_ports.description: The MIDI output port (None, Breakout, Select Bus, USB, Internal).
